meaning: adj.适于(或喜欢)漫游的,<美>(动物或人)四肢瘦长的
examples: A rangy middleweight, he fought in Cameroon and Senegal.
Manu Brabo, a rangy Spanish photographer, also had a ready grin.
The architect is Rick Mather, an experienced American Londoner for whom the term "rangy" might have been coined.
Rangy and broad-shouldered, he lent a rockabilly swagger to even his most technical briefings.
If Clementi had a touch of middle-aged fastidiousness, Ravi was fully a teen-ager: rangy, physical, with a taste for public regard.
